title: AMD's former pound-for-pound gaming champ hits its historic low again Ryzen 7 9800X3D offers unmatched value for gamers at $429
summary: The Ryzen 7 9800X3D has hit an all-time low price, making it a more affordable option for gamers than the more expensive Ryzen 7 9850X3D. This historic low price makes the Ryzen 7 9800X3D a more enticing choice for those looking for high-performance gaming at a lower cost. The price drop offers unmatched value for gamers.
insight: Historic price drop offers unmatched gaming value for budget-conscious gamers.

title: Invisible malicious code attacks 151 GitHub repos and VS Code Glassworm attack uses blockchain to steal tokens, credentials, and secrets
summary: Malicious code exploits Unicode Private Use Area characters to evade detection, targeting 151 GitHub repositories and using the VS Code Glassworm attack to steal sensitive information via blockchain. This technique renders as zero-width whitespace in most code editors and terminals. Attackers can secretly insert malicious code into projects without being detected.
insight: Evading detection via Unicode characters compromises security in most code environments.

title: MSI MAG B850 Tomahawk Max Wifi II Motherboard review: Sharpening the Tomahawk s blade
summary: The MSI MAG B850 Tomahawk Max Wifi II updates the budget AM5 board with new features, including additional M.2 storage and an OC Engine chip, while maintaining a competitive price. This iteration offers improved performance and capabilities without breaking the bank. Overall, the B850 Tomahawk Max Wifi II refines the original design for enhanced user experience.
insight: Budget enthusiasts get more value with refined AM5 motherboard features.

title: US gov't revokes controversial AI hardware export rule that would mandate investments from foreign companies new export rules are still in the works, though
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce withdrew a draft rule requiring foreign investments in U.S. AI sector as a condition for exporting AI accelerators. This move revokes a controversial rule that would give the U.S. government significant control over export controls. The department is still working on new export rules.
insight: U.S. maintains flexibility in AI export control policies.

title: Chocolate 3D printer startup Cocoa Press levels up with former Prusa executive
summary: Cocoa Press, a chocolate 3D printer startup, hires David Randolph as CEO. Randolph brings 10 years of manufacturing experience, including a stint as an executive at Prusa. The move aims to propel Cocoa Press to new heights.
insight: Manufacturing expertise boosts chocolate 3D printer startup's growth prospects significantly.

title: Noctua teases upcoming PC case with brown color scheme and bundled fans appears to be Antec Flux Pro Noctua Edition with NF-A14x25 G2 fans
summary: Noctua unveils a custom PC case with brown color scheme, featuring enhanced cooling hardware, mesh airflow panels, and support for large radiators. The case appears to be the Antec Flux Pro Noctua Edition. It comes bundled with NF-A14x25 G2 fans.
insight: High-end custom PC cases with enhanced cooling options emerge.

title: Chip material prices double as Middle East conflict compounds China's existing gallium export ban wide range of materials for chipmaking skyrocket as supply constricts
summary: Chip manufacturers face price hikes due to Middle East conflict disruptions and supply constraints, including China's gallium export ban.
insight: Disrupted global supply chains imperil chip production and tech industry stability.

title: Fiber internet provider says it can detect leaking water pipes using existing infrastructure, prevented loss of 2 million liters a day over three months Lightsonic tech detects underground vibrations, machine learning isolates the source
summary: Openreach used Lightsonic's tech to detect leaks, saving 2 million liters of water daily through existing fiber infrastructure.
insight: Early detection of leaks prevents massive water waste and saves resources.

title: DoJ dismantles botnet made of 360,000 infected routers and IOT devices spread across 163 countries that ran for 16 years SocksEscort proxy network eliminated in joint operation with Europol
summary: A 16-year-old botnet of 360,000 infected devices was dismantled in a joint US-EU operation with Europol, eliminating the SocksEscort proxy network that spread across 163 countries.
insight: Persistent, long-running cyber threats require sustained international cooperation and effort.

title: CPU fraud scandal erupts as another Chinese laptop busted with a fake chip second device sporting a disguised Ryzen 5500U uncovered after vendor threatens legal action
summary: Notebookcheck has uncovered a Chuwi laptop with allegedly fake processor, raising authenticity concerns.
insight: Fake CPUs undermine trust and fairness in the laptop market ecosystem.

title: Enthusiast rebuilds AA-battery-powered PC, sextuples run time to 30 minutes with 64 batteries uses three voltage regulators in parallel to achieve stability, runs computer for over 30 minutes on 64 AA cells
summary: YouTube creator ScuffedBits rebuilt their AA-battery-powered PC, achieving 30 minutes of game time. They used 64 AA batteries and three voltage regulators in parallel for stability. This impressive feat demonstrates the potential for creative battery-powered computing.
insight: Unconventional battery configurations can extend device run times significantly.

title: Six-month rewritable DVD endurance test crowns winner with 1,000 rewrites, shows the best discs are no longer manufactured six month of tests find TDK is a clear leader, Verbatim and Memorex didn t do well
summary: A six-month DVD rewritable endurance test found TDK as the top performer with 1,000 rewrites, outlasting other brands such as Verbatim and Memorex. The test showed the best discs are no longer manufactured, revealing concerns about the reliability of these products. This test highlights the importance of choosing a reliable DVD brand.
insight: Durability matters in rewritable DVDs, especially for long-term data storage.

title: US and Gulf states race for Ukrainian interceptor drones, 3D printed model costs $1,000 apiece Shahed-136 kamikaze drone threat spurs rush for interceptors
summary: Ukraine shares drone interceptors and training with the US and Gulf states to counter Iran's Shahed-136 kamikaze drones, as the Iran War escalates.
insight: Regional security at risk from Iran's drone warfare capabilities.

title: AERIS-10 open-source hardware radar can track multiple objects up to 20km away
summary: AERIS-10 is an open-source, low-cost 10.5 GHz phased array radar system, available in two versions. The AERIS-10X offers up to 20km range with a 32 16 dielectric-filled slotted waveguide array, while the AERIS-10N provides up to 3km range. The system features advanced processing and features like Doppler and CFAR processing.
insight: Open-source radar technology democratizes access to advanced tracking capabilities.

title: Survey finds Americans worry about AI data centers, but still want the jobs
summary: A survey found Americans are divided on data centers' environmental impact, but overwhelmingly believe they will create jobs and contribute to tax revenue.
insight: Employment concerns outweigh environmental concerns for AI data centers.

title: Drone strikes halt a third of the world's helium supply, threatening chip production
summary: Drone strikes have halted a third of the world's helium supply, impacting chip production. Helium is crucial for cooling silicon wafers in fabrication. Its lack of substitutes makes disruptions particularly significant.
insight: Disruption to helium supply can ripple through global chip manufacturing.

title: Pagers and paper maps make a comeback in Moscow as the internet goes dark
summary: Mobile internet has been intermittently failing in central Moscow and St. Petersburg, disrupting basic services.
insight: Disruption of internet access highlights society's reliance on modern technology.

title: Nvidia, AMD, and Intel back Microsoft's plan to fix shader compilation stutter
summary: Nvidia and AMD support Microsoft's Advanced Shader Delivery technology to fix shader-compilation stutter in PC games. The feature will arrive on Nvidia RTX graphics cards later this year. AMD has also confirmed its support.
insight: Reducing shader-compilation stutter improves smooth gaming experiences.
